name: posthog
title: PostHog product analytics

files.reason: Workers-safe PostHog client (flush immediately so events are not lost on isolate shutdown)

nextActions: Set POSTHOG_PROJECT_TOKEN and POSTHOG_HOST (wrangler.jsonc vars or secrets)
In a route: const ph = createPostHog(env); ctx.waitUntil(ph.captureImmediate({ distinctId, event: "..." })); ctx.waitUntil(ph.shutdown());
doc:
# Flarecel catalog: PostHog (Cloudflare Workers)

Uses `posthog-node`, which ships a `workerd` export and does not require `nodejs_compat` on its own. Create one client per request and flush immediately (`flushAt:1`, `flushInterval:0`) — batched data can be lost when a Worker isolate shuts down. Use `ctx.waitUntil(posthog.captureImmediate(...))` and `ctx.waitUntil(posthog.shutdown())` so capture completes without blocking the response.

Verified against PostHog's official Cloudflare Workers docs.
